strengths: Extensive TypeScript and Node.js expertise across multiple production environments and companies
Demonstrated full-stack capability with React, REST APIs, and infrastructure experience (Docker, AWS, Kubernetes)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, software craftsmanship, and clean architecture focus evident across projects
concerns: Severe overqualification—currently Staff-level engineer applying for entry-level role creates significant retention and frustration risk
Zero domain expertise in construction technology, renewable energy, energy consulting, or building renovation workflows
Recent experience emphasizes AI infrastructure and developer tooling; lacks focus on customer-facing application development this role requires

key_factors.factor: Technology focus shift (infrastructure/tooling → relational DB/product development)

response.justification: The candidate is dramatically overqualified for an entry-level individual contributor position, presenting substantial retention risk and likely frustration with career trajectory. While technical fundamentals (TypeScript, full-stack development, REST APIs, architectural patterns) satisfy the job requirements, the career progression—currently Staff engineer—indicates this role represents a step backward. More critically, the candidate lacks any domain knowledge in construction technology, renewable energy, or the CRM/document management integrations central to the role, and recent experience prioritizes AI infrastructure and developer tooling rather than the customer-facing application development the position requires.
